race.name: Boden Ski Marathon
race.tagline: Forty-five kilometers through the garrison town's boreal forests — Norrbotten skiing with military precision and Arctic grit.

vitals.aid_stations: 3 stations with warm drinks and energy food

climate.description: Boden's position in interior Norrbotten produces cold, stable winters with reliable snow cover from November through April. March temperatures typically -15°C to -5°C. Dry continental air from the east keeps snow quality excellent.
challenges: Consistent cold temperatures require proper layering
Wind exposure on open sections near military training grounds
Remote northern location adds travel logistics
Early March daylight improving but still limited

course.primary: Loop course through the boreal pine and spruce forests surrounding Boden, utilizing the garrison town's extensive trail network. Gently rolling terrain with no major climbs, typical of the Norrbotten interior plateau.
course.surface: Groomed classic track through boreal forest
course.grooming: Well-maintained by Boden Sports Federation, reliable quality
features: Trail network maintained partly by Swedish military garrison
Boreal forest setting with gentle Norrbotten terrain
Boden Fortress — historic military heritage backdrop
Reliable snow conditions in northern Sweden

history.summary: Founded in 1978 in Boden, Sweden's largest garrison town, where the military presence has maintained extensive trail infrastructure. Boden Fortress, built in the early 1900s as Sweden's northern defense, provides a unique historical backdrop. The marathon has become Norrbotten's accessible alternative to more remote Arctic races.
notable_facts: Boden is Sweden's largest garrison town — military skiing has deep roots here
Boden Fortress was Sweden's most expensive military construction, declassified in 1998
The town sits at the confluence of the Lule River
Norrbotten county is Sweden's largest by area, smallest by population density

youtube_data.search_query: Boden ski marathon cross country Norrbotten Sweden
